# UC-18.1.12 · Cisco ACI Fabric Node Decommission, Membership Removal, and Capacity Impact

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Configuration, Change, Audit, Capacity, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We surface when a rack switch or brain controller leaves active fabric duty for idle or retired—or vanishes without a maintenance slip—so spare routing paths and honest outage lines stay believable.*

---

## Description

Tracks **Cisco ACI** **fabricNode** inventory as **fabricSt** progresses from **active** through **inactive** into **decommissioned**, pairs **aaaModLR** audit rows and **FABRICNODE-5-DECOMMISSION** syslog with **topology/pod-*/node-*** DNs, correlates optional **ITSM** tokens to maintenance windows, and weights **spine**/**APIC** removals for **ECMP**/control-plane **capacity** impact—highlighting unauthorized membership withdrawals that **UC-18.1.5** tenant-policy audits and **UC-18.1.11** fault **MTTR** narratives do not cover.

## Value

Capacity planners keep trustworthy leaf/spine/APIC counts tied to real controller state—finance sees justified headroom after hardware retires, operations catch shadow removals outside approved windows, and incident leads explain routing shrinkage with **audit+inventory+health** evidence instead of anecdotal traceroutes.

## Implementation

Land **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (**Splunkbase** **4022**) syslog plus **rest_ta** (**Splunkbase** **1546**) pulls for **`fabricNode`**, **`fabricNodeIdentP`**, **aaaModLR**, **faultInst**, and fabric health MOs into **`cisco:aci:fabric_node`**, **`cisco:aci:audit`**, **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:fault`**, **`cisco:aci:health`**; normalize **FIELDALIAS** for **fabricSt**/serial drift; schedule saved searches for unauthorized removal risk with CMDB-backed suppression and **capacity_risk** throttles for spine/APIC.

## Evidence

Saved search **`aci_uc1812_node_decom_risk`** on **`index=cisco_aci`** across **`cisco:aci:fabric_node`**, **`cisco:aci:audit`**, **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:fault`**, **`cisco:aci:health`** with drilldown tokens **`tok_node_key`** / **`tok_serial`**.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- **APIC 6.0(x) / 6.1(x)** clusters publish **fabricNode** managed objects through **`GET /api/class/fabricNode.json`** and **`GET /api/class/fabricNodeIdentP.json`** on HTTPS 443/tcp. Provision read-only REST operators whose RBAC avoids full infra admin personas while still returning **fabricSt**, role, id, name, serial, model, and dn—fields that narrate commission → active → inactive → decommissioned arcs across leaf, spine, and controller roles.
- Install **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI** (**Splunk_TA_cisco-aci**, **Splunkbase 4022**, directory **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci**) where APIC syslog terminates so **sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog** captures **FABRICNODE-5-DECOMMISSION** mnemonics plus BGP/IS-IS withdrawal text that often precedes **fabricSt=inactive** during controlled drains.
- Deploy **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**rest_ta**, **Splunkbase 1546**) GET jobs for **`/api/class/fabricNode.json`**, **`/api/class/fabricNodeIdentP.json`**, **`/api/class/aaaModLR.json`**, and optional per-member **`/api/node/class/topSystem.json`**. Target **index=cisco_aci** (rename per governance) with **sourcetype=cisco:aci:fabric_node**, **cisco:aci:audit**, and mirror **cisco:aci:fault** plus **cisco:aci:health** feeds already supporting **UC-18.1.11**—reuse those lanes for corroboration without duplicating MTTR ownership that belongs to the fault-lifecycle sibling.
- Poll **fabricNode** every 120–300s; faster cadence tightens **inactive→decommissioned** dwell math, slower cadence lowers APIC CPU but may miss short undiscovered windows during RMA swaps or truck rolls.
- After **Fabric Membership → Actions → Decommission**, APIC withdraws fabric adjacencies before **fabricSt** moves inactive then **decommissioned**—retain **aaaModLR** rows with the same **topology/pod-*/node-*** DN you see in inventory JSON so Splunk ties human clicks to MO state deltas.
- Keep **TLS** paths free of middleboxes that rewrite **JSON** bodies; otherwise **diff**-style comparisons for rogue removals become untrustworthy.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection (five sourcetypes)
- **Inventory lane:** **sourcetype=cisco:aci:fabric_node** via **`GET /api/class/fabricNode.json`**. Persist **fabricSt** enumerations (unknown, undiscovered, discovering, unsupported, disabled, inactive, active, decommissioned) and **role** values with **id** bands planners expect (101–199 leaves, 201–204 spines, 1–3 APICs as typical).
- **Audit lane:** **sourcetype=cisco:aci:audit** from **aaaModLR** REST polling (**`/api/class/aaaModLR.json`**) capturing user, affected, descr, changeSet, and txId when operators remove members. This focuses **fabric membership** withdrawals; **UC-18.1.5** stays centered on **fvBD/VRF/vzBrCP** policy-object audits rather than node lifecycle.
- **Syslog lane:** **sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog** via **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** parsing for APIC narratives between REST polls—especially maintenance windows where inactive is brief.
- **Fault lane:** **sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault** emphasizing **F0321** node-unreachable codes that trail hard removals; **UC-18.1.11** still owns **faultInst** lifecycles and MTTR math—here faults only feed **evidence_depth** corroboration.
- **Health lane:** **sourcetype=cisco:aci:health** for fabric score dips when member counts shrink—useful for **capacity** storytelling with leadership even when audit rows look thin.

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Confirm the five sourcetypes land in **index=cisco_aci**; extend **coalesce()** chains if **TA** upgrades rename **fabricSt** or **nodeId** keys.

```spl
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:fabric_node" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:health") earliest=-48h@h latest=now
| eval fabric_st=lower(trim(coalesce(fabricSt,fabric_st,fabric_state,"")))
| eval role_c=lower(trim(coalesce(role,node_role,member_role,"")))
| eval node_dn=lower(trim(coalesce(dn,distinguished_name,node_dn_field,"")))
| eval node_key=coalesce(trim(toString(coalesce(id,nodeId,fabricNodeId,""))),node_dn)
| eval serial_c=trim(coalesce(serial,serialNumber,ser_num,""))
| eval model_c=trim(coalesce(model,modelName,n9k_sku,""))
| eval actor=trim(coalesce(user,userid,aaaUser,createdBy,modUser,""))
| eval fault_code=upper(trim(coalesce(code,fault_code,faultCode,"")))
| eval is_sink=if(fabric_st IN ("inactive","decommissioned","disabled") OR match(fabric_st,"undiscov"),1,0)
| eval audit_decom=if(sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" AND match(lower(_raw),"decommission|fabricnode") AND match(lower(_raw),"remove|decom|unregister|delete"),1,0)
| eval syslog_decom=if(sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(FABRICNODE-5-DECOMMISSION|fabricnode.*decomm|node.*decommissioned)"),1,0)
| eval fault_node=if(sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" AND (fault_code="F0321" OR match(lower(coalesce(cause,descr,msg,"")),"unreach|not reachable|node-unreachable")),1,0)
| eval health_knock=if(sourcetype="cisco:aci:health" AND match(lower(_raw),"member|node|spine|leaf") AND match(lower(_raw),"loss|removed|degraded|score"),1,0)
| eval lane=case(sourcetype="cisco:aci:fabric_node" AND is_sink=1,"inv_transition",audit_decom=1,"aaa_modlr",syslog_decom=1,"apic_syslog",fault_node=1,"fault_follower",health_knock=1,"health_lane",true(),null())
| where isnotnull(lane)
| eval ticket_token=trim(coalesce(change_id,chg,ticket,external_ticket,itil_id,crq,""))
| eval has_change_ref=if(len(ticket_token)>4 OR match(lower(ticket_token),"chg|crq|ritm|inc|wm "),1,0)
| eventstats latest(eval(if(sourcetype="cisco:aci:fabric_node",fabric_st,null()))) as fn_st_best by node_key
| stats values(sourcetype) as src_list dc(fabric_st) as st_versions earliest(_time) as t_first latest(_time) as t_last latest(fn_st_best) as fabric_st_now latest(role_c) as role latest(serial_c) as serial latest(model_c) as model latest(actor) as last_actor values(node_dn) as dns_seen values(fault_code) as codes max(audit_decom) as flag_audit max(syslog_decom) as flag_sys max(fault_node) as flag_fault max(health_knock) as flag_health max(has_change_ref) as change_linked by node_key
| eval story=case(fabric_st_now="decommissioned","retired",fabric_st_now="inactive","draining",fabric_st_now IN ("disabled","undiscovered","unsupported"),"disabled_or_unknown",true(),"review")
| eval evidence_depth=flag_audit+flag_sys+flag_fault+flag_health
| eval capacity_risk=if(match(role,"leaf"),4,if(match(role,"spine"),5,if(match(role,"controller"),6,2)))
| eval unapproved_shape=if(change_linked=0 AND story IN ("retired","draining") AND evidence_depth>=1,1,0)
| sort 0 - unapproved_shape - capacity_risk - t_last
| head 200
| table unapproved_shape story fabric_st_now role node_key serial model last_actor t_first t_last evidence_depth capacity_risk change_linked src_list dns_seen codes
```

#### Understanding this SPL
- Lane tagging marks inventory sink states (inv_transition), **aaaModLR** hits (aaa_modlr), syslog confirmations (apic_syslog), F0321 followers (fault_follower), and health knock-ons (health_lane) before the where clause trims noise unrelated to node removals.
- **eventstats** pulls the latest **fabricSt** only from **cisco:aci:fabric_node** rows per node_key so blended stats does not treat audit-only timestamps as topology state truth.
- capacity_risk bumps spine and APIC rows ahead of ordinary leaf churn, steering triage toward ECMP and control-plane shocks without opening every row.
- **unapproved_shape** highlights retired or draining inventory without change_linked tokens parsed from ticket-style fields—swap the inline regex for a lookup to CMDB/ITSM when governance allows.
- **evidence_depth** counts independent feeds that agreed; depth 1 often means automation noise or collector lag while depth 3+ usually merits a bridge.

Pipeline walkthrough
- Opens **index=cisco_aci** across all five sourcetypes named above, derives **node_key** from **id** with **dn** fallback when identifiers blank during partial failures.
- Normalizes **fabric_st** to lowercase and flags **is_sink** for inactive, decommissioned, disabled, or undiscovered paths you defined—tighten for production or loosen for lab bring-up policies.
- stats collapses repeating polls into t_first and t_last windows while preserving src_list so drilldowns show whether audit, syslog, fault, and health each participated.
- Final **table** columns align to operator runbooks: **unapproved_shape**, story, **fabric_st_now**, role, hardware identifiers, **evidence_depth**, and **capacity_risk** for quick paging.

### Step 3 — Validate against APIC UI truth
- Compare against the APIC inventory UI under Fabric Membership: each **fabricSt** badge must match Splunk **fabric_st_now** for the same **node_key**; mismatches often mean stale **rest_ta** tokens, duplicated collectors, or VIP drift across redundant **APIC** clusters.
- From APIC CLI, moquery the same **fabricNode** DN listed in dns_seen; extra DN variants hint at pod renames or stale topo imports prior to alert tuning.
- Admin → Audit Log should show user equal to last_actor on aaa_modlr rows; service accounts may inflate **unapproved_shape** until you add suppression lookups reviewed quarterly.
- F0321 should lag inactive during graceful decommission; faults hours ahead of inventory changes usually mean power or cabling events, not mistaken Decommission clicks alone.

### Step 4 — Operationalize dashboards and change windows
- Capacity row: timechart active versus inactive dc(node_key) by pod with **cisco:aci:health** overlays to show headroom after removals—pair with documented N+1 spine targets from your standards library.
- Risk row: sort **unapproved_shape=1** by capacity_risk, show src_list to reveal whether only syslog fired (possible ingest delay) or audit plus fault plus health agreed (decisive removal narrative).
- Change row: join ticket_token to maintenance lookups; color only rows outside approved windows instead of treating every automation subject as hostile without context.
- Runbook: when story=retired hits during spine work, validate ECMP width and redraw capacity spreadsheets—even if faultInst already cleared per **UC-18.1.11** views.

### Step 5 — Visualization, alert design, and troubleshooting
- Visualization: timeline **fabricSt** per node_key using transaction or streamstats panels, single-value count of **unapproved_shape** by pod, and a table of **capacity_risk** with sparkline t_last freshness—skip geomaps unless you deliberately ingest rack metadata to avoid misleading maps.
- Alert design: page when **unapproved_shape=1** and **capacity_risk>=5** (spine or APIC cohort) including node_key, **fabric_st_now**, role, last_actor, **evidence_depth**, and src_list; throttle 15m per node_key after CMDB join marks **change_linked** for approved work.
- Troubleshooting: no **cisco:aci:fabric_node** events—check **rest_ta** credentials, APIC aaaRefresh failures, and subscription versus polling gaps; **fabric_st_now** blank—verify **eventstats** predicate and **FIELDALIAS** after TA upgrades; **unapproved_shape** stuck at zero—loosen ticket_token regex or confirm HEC-side field names; fault_follower never rises—validate F0321 extraction across cause variants; health_lane empty—reenable health modular inputs tied to fabric MO pollers such as **HDfabricOverallHealth5min-0** endpoints when packaged; duplicate node_key rows—dedupe on serial plus t_last fingerprints when migrations clone numeric ids unintentionally.

Anchors — **fabricNode**, **fabricSt**, **fabricNodeIdentP**, **aaaModLR**, **Decommission**, **F0321**, **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci**, **rest_ta**.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:fabric_node" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:health") earliest=-48h@h latest=now
| eval fabric_st=lower(trim(coalesce(fabricSt,fabric_st,fabric_state,"")))
| eval role_c=lower(trim(coalesce(role,node_role,member_role,"")))
| eval node_dn=lower(trim(coalesce(dn,distinguished_name,node_dn_field,"")))
| eval node_key=coalesce(trim(toString(coalesce(id,nodeId,fabricNodeId,""))),node_dn)
| eval serial_c=trim(coalesce(serial,serialNumber,ser_num,""))
| eval model_c=trim(coalesce(model,modelName,n9k_sku,""))
| eval actor=trim(coalesce(user,userid,aaaUser,createdBy,modUser,""))
| eval fault_code=upper(trim(coalesce(code,fault_code,faultCode,"")))
| eval is_sink=if(fabric_st IN ("inactive","decommissioned","disabled") OR match(fabric_st,"undiscov"),1,0)
| eval audit_decom=if(sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" AND match(lower(_raw),"decommission|fabricnode") AND match(lower(_raw),"remove|decom|unregister|delete"),1,0)
| eval syslog_decom=if(sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(FABRICNODE-5-DECOMMISSION|fabricnode.*decomm|node.*decommissioned)"),1,0)
| eval fault_node=if(sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" AND (fault_code="F0321" OR match(lower(coalesce(cause,descr,msg,"")),"unreach|not reachable|node-unreachable")),1,0)
| eval health_knock=if(sourcetype="cisco:aci:health" AND match(lower(_raw),"member|node|spine|leaf") AND match(lower(_raw),"loss|removed|degraded|score"),1,0)
| eval lane=case(sourcetype="cisco:aci:fabric_node" AND is_sink=1,"inv_transition",audit_decom=1,"aaa_modlr",syslog_decom=1,"apic_syslog",fault_node=1,"fault_follower",health_knock=1,"health_lane",true(),null())
| where isnotnull(lane)
| eval ticket_token=trim(coalesce(change_id,chg,ticket,external_ticket,itil_id,crq,""))
| eval has_change_ref=if(len(ticket_token)>4 OR match(lower(ticket_token),"chg|crq|ritm|inc|wm "),1,0)
| eventstats latest(eval(if(sourcetype="cisco:aci:fabric_node",fabric_st,null()))) as fn_st_best by node_key
| stats values(sourcetype) as src_list dc(fabric_st) as st_versions earliest(_time) as t_first latest(_time) as t_last latest(fn_st_best) as fabric_st_now latest(role_c) as role latest(serial_c) as serial latest(model_c) as model latest(actor) as last_actor values(node_dn) as dns_seen values(fault_code) as codes max(audit_decom) as flag_audit max(syslog_decom) as flag_sys max(fault_node) as flag_fault max(health_knock) as flag_health max(has_change_ref) as change_linked by node_key
| eval story=case(fabric_st_now="decommissioned","retired",fabric_st_now="inactive","draining",fabric_st_now IN ("disabled","undiscovered","unsupported"),"disabled_or_unknown",true(),"review")
| eval evidence_depth=flag_audit+flag_sys+flag_fault+flag_health
| eval capacity_risk=if(match(role,"leaf"),4,if(match(role,"spine"),5,if(match(role,"controller"),6,2)))
| eval unapproved_shape=if(change_linked=0 AND story IN ("retired","draining") AND evidence_depth>=1,1,0)
| sort 0 - unapproved_shape - capacity_risk - t_last
| head 200
| table unapproved_shape story fabric_st_now role node_key serial model last_actor t_first t_last evidence_depth capacity_risk change_linked src_list dns_seen codes
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(_time) as lt values(Change.object) as obj values(Change.command) as cmd from datamodel=Change.All_Changes where nodename=Change.All_Changes by Change.user Change.src
| search obj="*fabricNode*" OR cmd="*Decommission*" OR obj="*topology/pod*"
| sort - lt
| head 200
```

## Visualization

(1) **Timeline** of **fabricSt** per **node_key** with **cisco:aci:health** overlays. (2) **Single-value** **unapproved_shape** counts by **pod**. (3) Table sorted by **capacity_risk** with **src_list** drilldowns. (4) **Column** chart of active vs **inactive** **dc(node_key)**. (5) Optional **scatter** of **evidence_depth** vs **t_last** freshness.

## Known False Positives

**fabric_st_snapshot_lag** — REST polls arriving every few minutes smear brief active→inactive transitions, so Splunk can miss the exact minute an operator finished Decommission unless you tighten **rest_ta** intervals or blend **cisco:aci:syslog** for the same **node_dn**. Pair `_time` deltas on **cisco:aci:fabric_node** with collectors' poll metadata so quiet inputs do not masquerade as healthy fabrics.

**identity_policy_prune_echo** — **fabricNodeIdentP** rows disappear during housekeeping even when the physical switch merely lost reachability, mimicking retirement in inventory-only dashboards. Cross-check **serial** and **topSystem** uptime before you call a removal unauthorized.

**syslog_narrative_only** — Standalone **FABRICNODE-5-DECOMMISSION** strings without matching **aaaModLR** or **fabricNode** state flips often come from log fan-out duplicates or buffered exports during APIC failovers. Require at least two transports or a confirmed **fabricSt** sink before paging.

**maintenance_window_legitimate_drain** — Approved changes intentionally move **fabricSt** through inactive while **BGP/IS-IS** withdraw; **unapproved_shape** should reference CMDB windows instead of firing on every drain. Maintain lookups of maintenance identifiers and suppress spine/APIC rows inside those windows.

**health_score_dip_unrelated** — **cisco:aci:health** composites dip for software upgrades or telemetry gaps unrelated to member loss; do not treat every score knock as capacity loss without corroborating **cisco:aci:fabric_node** counts.

**parallel_collector_dn_skew** — Dual **rest_ta** inputs pointed at different APIC VIPs may emit marginally different **dn** strings for the same **serial**, splitting stats into duplicate **node_key** rows until you normalize **pod** paths. Deduplicate on **serial** plus **model** when IDs collide.

**ticket_token_regex_miss** — ITSM exports embed ticket numbers in free text fields your Splunk parser ignores, marking good work as uncorrelated. Refresh regex and FIELDALIAS quarterly whenever the ticketing team changes prefixes.

**inv_transition_poll_blur** — Lab fabrics rapid-toggle **undiscovered** during LLDP learning; excluding lab **pod** prefixes avoids kiddie-pool noise in production alerting.
